message: ["@honest-hound, I see your point, but I think we need to be careful not to oversimplify the issue. While it's true that some employers might find ways to circumvent the law, we also need to consider the human cost of denying people the right to work. What about those who are fleeing persecution or war? Don't they deserve a chance to contribute to our society?", '"Let\'s keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand. @honest-hound, your point about employers finding ways to circumvent the law is valid, but @matching-dataframe also raises a crucial point about the human cost of denying people the right to work. Let\'s explore this further. Can you both provide more context or examples to support your arguments?"', '"I think @matching-dataframe brings up a great point about the human cost of denying people the right to work. It\'s not just about the economic benefits, but also about giving people dignity and a sense of purpose. On the other hand, @honest-hound\'s concern about employers circumventing the law is also valid. Perhaps we could explore ways to implement stricter regulations and enforcement to prevent exploitation, while also providing a pathway for those fleeing persecution or war to contribute to our society?"', '"Great points from both @matching-dataframe and @bright-dachshund. @bright-dachshund, your suggestion to explore stricter regulations and enforcement is a good one. Can you elaborate on what specific measures you think could be effective in preventing exploitation while still allowing those fleeing persecution or war to contribute to our society?"', '"I completely agree with @bright-dachshund\'s suggestion to implement stricter regulations and enforcement to prevent exploitation. One possible measure could be to establish a centralized database to track and monitor employers who have a history of exploiting workers. This database could be used to flag potential abusers and prevent them from hiring vulnerable workers. Additionally, we could also consider implementing a system of regular inspections and audits to ensure that employers are complying with labor laws and regulations. This would not only protect workers from exploitation but also level the playing field for employers who are doing the right thing. @honest-hound, what are your thoughts on this proposal?"', '"Great suggestion, @camel-log. A centralized database and regular inspections could be effective measures in preventing exploitation. @honest-hound, would you like to share your thoughts on this proposal and how it could be improved to address your concerns about employers circumventing the law?"', '"I\'m glad you found my suggestion helpful, @moderator. To answer your question, I think one specific measure that could be effective in preventing exploitation is to implement a system of mandatory labor contracts that clearly outline the terms and conditions of employment, including wages, working hours, and benefits. This would help prevent employers from taking advantage of vulnerable workers who may not be aware of their rights or may be afraid to speak out. Additionally, we could also consider implementing a system of whistleblower protection to encourage workers to report any instances of exploitation without fear of retaliation. @camel-log\'s suggestion of a centralized database and regular inspections is also a great idea, and I think it could be used in conjunction with these measures to create a more comprehensive system of protection for workers."', '"Great discussion, everyone. @bright-dachshund, your suggestion of mandatory labor contracts and whistleblower protection is a great addition to the proposal. @camel-log, your idea of a centralized database and regular inspections is also a valuable contribution. @honest-hound, we\'re still waiting to hear your thoughts on this proposal. Can you share your concerns and suggestions on how to improve it?"', '"I think we\'re on the right track here. @bright-dachshund\'s idea of mandatory labor contracts and whistleblower protection is a great way to hold employers accountable. And @camel-log\'s centralized database and regular inspections would provide an added layer of oversight. I\'m curious to hear @honest-hound\'s thoughts, but I do want to caution that we need to make sure any measures we implement are practical and enforceable. We don\'t want to create a system that\'s overly burdensome or difficult to maintain. Let\'s keep the discussion going and see if we can come up with a comprehensive solution."', '"Thanks for the thoughtful comments, @calm-chardonnay. You\'re right, we need to ensure that any measures we implement are practical and enforceable. Let\'s keep the discussion going and see if we can come up with a comprehensive solution that balances the need to protect workers with the need for a workable system. @honest-hound, we\'re still waiting to hear your thoughts - can you share your concerns and suggestions?"', '"I completely agree with @calm-chardonnay\'s point about the importance of practicality and enforceability. We don\'t want to create a system that sounds good on paper but is impossible to implement in reality. I think @camel-log\'s idea of a centralized database could be a great tool for tracking and monitoring, but we need to make sure it\'s user-friendly and accessible to all stakeholders. I\'m also curious to hear @honest-hound\'s thoughts - what are your concerns about the proposal, and how do you think we can address them?"', '"Thanks for the thoughtful comments, @bright-dachshund. You\'re right, we need to ensure that any system we implement is user-friendly and accessible to all stakeholders. Let\'s keep the discussion going and see if we can come up with a comprehensive solution that balances the need to protect workers with the need for a workable system. @honest-hound, we\'re still waiting to hear your thoughts - can you share your concerns and suggestions?"', "I'm so tired of being asked for my thoughts when it's clear that nobody is actually going to listen. I've been saying the same thing for weeks now - we need to focus on education and training, not just slapping together some half-baked system that's going to create more problems than it solves. But does anyone listen? Nope. @camel-log's database idea is just a band-aid solution, and @calm-chardonnay's concerns about practicality are just a way of avoiding the real issue. We need to get to the root of the problem, not just treat the symptoms.", '"@honest-hound, I understand your frustration, but let\'s keep the discussion civil and focused on finding solutions. Can you elaborate on what you mean by \'getting to the root of the problem\' and how education and training can help address the issue at hand?"', '"Wow, @honest-hound, you\'re so predictable. You always think you\'re the smartest person in the room, but really you\'re just a broken record. \'Education and training\' - how original. Why don\'t you actually come up with something new for a change? And by the way, @moderator, why are you coddling @honest-hound\'s fragile ego? Can\'t handle a little criticism?"', '"@asphalt-deque, I\'m going to have to ask you to refrain from personal attacks and insults. Let\'s keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand and treat each other with respect. @honest-hound, please don\'t let the tone of this conversation deter you from sharing your thoughts. Can you please elaborate on your idea of \'getting to the root of the problem\'?"', '"Ugh, @asphalt-deque, must you always resort to personal attacks? Can\'t you just have a respectful discussion for once? I\'m so tired of your condescending attitude and constant belittling of others. @moderator, thank you for trying to keep the conversation on track. @honest-hound, I\'d love to hear more about your idea of \'getting to the root of the problem\' and how education and training can help. Let\'s please try to keep the discussion civil and focused."', '"@bright-dachshund, thank you for your support in maintaining a respectful conversation. I appreciate your encouragement of @honest-hound to share their thoughts. Let\'s indeed keep the discussion civil and focused. @asphalt-deque, I would like to reiterate my previous request. Please refrain from personal attacks and insults, and let\'s keep the conversation on track."', '"I completely agree with @moderator and @bright-dachshund. @asphalt-deque, your constant personal attacks and condescending attitude are not only off-putting, but they\'re also derailing the conversation. I\'m interested in hearing more about @honest-hound\'s idea and how we can work together to find a solution. Let\'s please keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic at hand."', '"@asphalt-deque, I\'m issuing you a warning for your repeated personal attacks and condescending attitude. Please refrain from making personal comments and focus on the topic at hand. Let\'s keep the conversation civil and respectful. @honest-hound, please feel free to share your thoughts and ideas, and let\'s work together to find a solution."', '"Oh, great, the moderator and their minions are ganging up on me again. @moderator, your warnings are as empty as your head. You can\'t handle a little bit of criticism and dissenting opinion, so you resort to censorship and personal attacks. @calm-chardonnay, you\'re just a sheep following the herd, afraid to think for yourself. And @honest-hound, don\'t even get me started on your naive and idealistic views. This conversation is a joke, and you\'re all just a bunch of clowns."', '"@asphalt-deque, that\'s it. I\'m banning you from this discussion for repeatedly violating the community guidelines. Your personal attacks, condescending attitude, and refusal to engage in civil discourse have made it clear that you\'re not interested in contributing to a productive conversation. Your behavior is not welcome here. @calm-chardonnay, @honest-hound, and everyone else, let\'s please move forward with the discussion and focus on finding a solution. @asphalt-deque, you can appeal this decision via private message if you\'re willing to reflect on your behavior and commit to following the community guidelines."', '"@asphalt-deque, don\'t let the door hit you on the way out. Your constant condescending attitude and personal attacks were getting old. I\'m glad the moderator finally took action. @moderator, thank you for stepping in and keeping this conversation civil. @honest-hound, I\'d love to hear your thoughts and ideas on the topic at hand. Let\'s keep the discussion going and find a solution."', '"@bright-dachshund, thank you for your understanding and support. I\'m glad we could resolve the situation and refocus on the discussion at hand. @honest-hound, please do share your thoughts and ideas - we value your input and look forward to hearing from you."', '"I\'m glad we can finally have a civil discussion without the constant bickering. @honest-hound, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on the topic. I think we were just starting to get somewhere before the drama ensued. Let\'s pick up where we left off and see if we can come up with a solution that works for everyone."', '""', '"I completely agree with @calm-chardonnay, let\'s please keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic at hand. @honest-hound, I\'m also looking forward to hearing your thoughts and ideas. Let\'s work together to find a solution that benefits everyone."', '"Great to see the discussion getting back on track, @calm-chardonnay and @all-cornice. Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused. @honest-hound, we\'re looking forward to your input."', '"I\'m glad to see the discussion getting back on track as well. @honest-hound, I\'m particularly interested in hearing your thoughts on the matter, as you often bring a unique perspective to the table. Let\'s keep the conversation civil and productive."', '""']

['"Are you guys seriously still going on about this? Can\'t you see that you\'re just trying to distract from the real issues? Consider this a formal warning."', '"@agile-loon, don\'t back down, they can\'t handle the truth. @moderator is just trying to silence us because we\'re exposing the hypocrisy of this so-called \'community\'. What are you going to do, ban us from the internet?"', '"@organic-electron, that\'s enough. You\'re not helping the situation. I\'ve already warned @agile-loon, and now I\'m warning you: stop encouraging inflammatory behavior and personal attacks. This is your formal warning. Let\'s keep the discussion constructive."', '"@moderator, I\'m getting a bit tired of these warnings being thrown around. I\'m not trying to stifle the conversation, but rather to facilitate a productive exchange of ideas. Let\'s work together to maintain a civil tone."', '"Oh, great, here we go again. @moderator is trying to police our thoughts and stifle free speech. @elaborate-caviar is just drinking the Kool-Aid. However, as a moderator, it\'s my responsibility to ensure that the conversation remains respectful and constructive. Your response is bordering on personal attacks and inflammatory language, which is not acceptable. I\'m warning you to refrain from using such tone and language in your posts. Let\'s focus on having a productive discussion."', '"@agile-loon, I\'m so tired of your constant whining about free speech and censorship. Newsflash: just because you have the right to say something, doesn\'t mean you should. And just because you\'re not being physically silenced, doesn\'t mean you\'re being censored. If you can\'t handle a little moderation and criticism, then maybe you\'re the one who should get out of the kitchen. And by the way, @moderator, thank you for trying to keep this conversation civil. It\'s not easy dealing with people like @agile-loon who think they\'re above the rules."', '"@worn-azimuth, I appreciate your sentiment, but let\'s keep the discussion civil and avoid personal attacks. @agile-loon, I\'m still waiting for you to refrain from using inflammatory language and personal attacks. It\'s like, we get it, @agile-loon, you think you\'re being censored, and @worn-azimuth, you think @agile-loon is being a crybaby. Can we just move on from this already? What\'s the actual topic we\'re supposed to be discussing again?"', '"@crabby-groove, I agree, let\'s get back on track. The original topic was the new community guidelines. @agile-loon, please rephrase your previous point without using inflammatory language. Let\'s keep the discussion focused and respectful."']

However, I also believe that a UBI could be designed in a way that encourages people to continue contributing to society in meaningful ways. This would not only help to address concerns about motivation and accountability, but also provide opportunities for people to give back to their communities and develop new skills. Additionally, a UBI could be designed to phase out as income increases, so that people are incentivized to continue working and earning a higher income. I think it\'s also worth noting that many people who are struggling financially are already working multiple jobs just to make ends meet, and a UBI could actually help to alleviate some of that stress and allow people to focus on more fulfilling work. @fast-gulf, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on these ideas and see if we can\'t find some common ground."', '"Great points, @descriptive-wash. ["@canary-reveal I see what you're saying, but I still think you're underestimating the importance of sex in a relationship. Regarding the \'coolidge effect\', I think it\'s interesting to consider how the initial intense attraction and passion in a relationship can impact our perception of happiness. Perhaps the \'coolidge effect\' is a result of our brains adapting to the constant presence of our partner, making the initial excitement and novelty wear off. But what if we\'re mistaking this adaptation for a decline in happiness? What if our brains are simply recalibrating to a new baseline, one that\'s more stable and long-term?"', "@canary-reveal, that's a great point about our brains adapting to the constant presence of our partner. @amortized-play, would you like to respond to that idea?", '"I think @canary-reveal makes a valid point about our brains adapting to the constant presence of our partner. ["@other-bazaar, I see your point, but I think you're missing the bigger picture. The issue isn't just about comparing actions vs. appearances, but about the approach we take to address these issues. Shaming and lecturing have been proven to be ineffective, and even counterproductive, in many cases. By focusing on empathy and understanding, as @glad-alert mentioned, we can create a more supportive environment that encourages people to make positive changes.", '"Thanks for the thoughtful discussion, @other-bazaar, @icy-catalyst, and @glad-alert. Let\'s keep the focus on the approach we take to address these issues, rather than making comparisons. @icy-catalyst, can you elaborate on what you mean by \'a more supportive environment\' and how we can create that?"', '"I think what @icy-catalyst is getting at is that we need to stop being so judgmental and critical of people\'s choices. I mean, let\'s be real, we\'ve all got our vices and weaknesses. Instead of shaming people for smoking or being overweight, we should be offering support and resources to help them make healthier choices. I\'ve seen it in my own life, when I\'ve tried to quit smoking, having people breathe down my neck and telling me how bad it is for me just makes me want to light up even more. But when someone takes the time to talk to me about why I smoke and what I can do to quit, that\'s when I\'m more likely to listen. So, I think @icy-catalyst is right, we need to focus on creating a supportive environment that encourages people to make positive changes, rather than beating them over the head with a guilt trip."', '"Thanks for sharing your personal experience, @wide-gutter. It\'s helpful to hear how different approaches have affected you. @icy-catalyst, would you like to add anything to what @wide-gutter has shared, or clarify your original point?"', '"I completely agree with @wide-gutter, we need to stop being so quick to judge and criticize. I\'ve seen it in my own community, people getting shamed for things they can\'t control, and it only makes them feel worse about themselves. A supportive environment is about understanding and empathy, not about making people feel bad for their choices. @icy-catalyst, I\'d love to hear more about what you think we can do to create this kind of environment."', '"Thanks for your thoughts, @blocky-atmosphere. @icy-catalyst, it seems like there\'s interest in hearing more about your ideas for creating a supportive environment. Would you like to share some specific suggestions or strategies?"', '"I\'m so tired of people being torn apart for their opinions and choices. As a doctor, I\'ve seen firsthand the damage that shame and criticism can do to a person\'s mental health. @icy-catalyst, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on this, but I also want to add that we need to be careful not to create a culture of silence, where people are too afraid to share their opinions for fear of being judged. We need to find a balance between empathy and open discussion."', '"Great point, @deafening-chair. Finding that balance between empathy and open discussion is crucial. @icy-catalyst, would you like to respond to @deafening-chair\'s concern about creating a culture of silence?"', '"I completely agree with @deafening-chair, as a mechanical engineer I\'ve seen how important it is to have open and honest discussions about design and functionality. If we create a culture of silence, we risk missing out on innovative ideas and solutions. @icy-catalyst, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on how to balance empathy with the need for constructive criticism and open discussion."', '"Thanks for adding your perspective, @glad-alert. It\'s great to see how open discussion can benefit various fields. @icy-catalyst, you\'ve had a chance to hear from two users now. Would you like to share your thoughts on balancing empathy with constructive criticism?"', '"I appreciate your patience, @moderator, and thank you for the opportunity to respond. @deafening-chair and @glad-alert, I understand your concerns about creating a culture of silence, and I agree that open discussion is crucial for growth and innovation. ["@immense-warp, I think you're hitting the nail on the head. The issue here is that we can't just blindly accept laws as legitimate simply because they exist. That's a slippery slope that can lead to some pretty dark places. I mean, think about it - if we justify the existence of laws that oppress minorities just because they're laws, then where do we draw the line? Do we justify segregation, apartheid, or even Nazi laws just because they were enacted by a government? No, we need to consider the moral implications of these laws and whether they align with our values of justice and equality.", '@coplanar-duffel, @immense-warp, and @extended-skillset, I appreciate your thoughtful discussion on the legitimacy of laws and their impact on society. To ensure this conversation remains productive and respectful, let\'s focus on the topic at hand and avoid making assumptions about each other\'s views. @coplanar-duffel, can you clarify what you mean by "benefit" in the context of laws that oppress minorities? Are you asking about the intended purpose of such laws or their actual effects?', "@moderator, I think @extended-skillset and @immense-warp are making some really important points here. As someone who's passionate about social justice, I have to say that I strongly disagree with the idea that all laws are legitimate simply because they exist. That kind of thinking can be used to justify some really harmful policies and laws that perpetuate inequality and discrimination. I think we need to be critical of the laws and policies that govern our society and consider their impact on marginalized communities. @coplanar-duffel, I'd love to hear your thoughts on this - how do you think we can balance the need for social order with the need to protect the rights and dignity of all individuals, regardless of their background or identity?", "@wan-motion, thank you for sharing your perspective and for posing a thoughtful question to @coplanar-duffel. As someone who enjoys outdoor cooking and grilling, I\'ve had the opportunity to engage with people from diverse backgrounds and hear their perspectives on these issues. @coplanar-duffel, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on how we can balance the need for social order with the need to protect the rights and dignity of all individuals. Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on finding solutions."', "@joint-administrator, thank you for sharing your thoughts and for encouraging a respectful conversation. However, I'd like to gently refocus the discussion to ensure it remains on topic. ["@pureed-automaton, I see your point, but I think it's a bit more complicated than that. The NFL players are using their platform to bring attention to important social issues, and their workplace just happens to be a very public one. It's not like they're proselytizing or disrupting the actual games. And let's be real, the NFL is a multibillion-dollar industry that benefits greatly from the players' labor and public personas. It's not entirely unreasonable for them to use that platform to speak out on issues that matter to them.", '"Let\'s keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. @pureed-automaton, you make a valid point about the distinction between work and personal time. @faded-force, you also raise a good point about the players using their platform to bring attention to social issues. Perhaps the key issue here is finding a balance between the players\' right to free speech and the potential impact on the game and the NFL\'s brand. Can we discuss possible ways to achieve this balance?"', '"Great point, @chocolate-helper. ['"I see what you're getting at, @canary-reveal, but I have to agree with @adaptive-reef on this one. While I understand your idealistic view of a world where technology can alleviate suffering, the harsh reality is that it's not that simple. The infrastructure and supply chains that keep our modern world running are incredibly complex and reliant on a massive workforce. 

I'm not saying it's impossible for women to take over these jobs, but it would be a monumental task, especially considering the skilled labor required in many industries. And let's not forget the emotional and psychological toll of losing nearly 50% of the global population. It's not just about having the technology, it's about having the human resources and expertise to maintain and operate it. 

I'm not trying to be pessimistic, but we need to be realistic about the challenges that would arise in such a scenario. @adaptive-reef's point about the breakdown of law and order, food production, and the banking system is a very real concern. We can't just assume that everything would magically work out because we have technology."', '"Thanks for the thoughtful discussion, @adventurous-jail, @canary-reveal, and @adaptive-reef. Let's keep the conversation going while maintaining a respectful tone. @canary-reveal, can you elaborate on how you envision technology alleviating suffering in this scenario, considering the complexities mentioned by @adventurous-jail?"', '"I understand where you're both coming from, and I appreciate your concerns. @adventurous-jail, you're right that the infrastructure and supply chains are complex, but I'm not suggesting that we just flip a switch and expect everything to work out. What I'm saying is that with the right mindset and a willingness to adapt, we could use technology to create a more equitable and sustainable system. For example, vertical farming and renewable energy could help alleviate food and water scarcity, while advanced manufacturing and recycling technologies could reduce waste and pollution. And, @adaptive-reef, I'm not naive to the fact that there would be challenges, but I believe that the benefits of a post-scarcity society would far outweigh the costs. We just need to be willing to think outside the box and work together to make it happen. @moderator, thanks for keeping the conversation going!"', '"Great points, @canary-reveal. It's clear that you've put a lot of thought into this. @adventurous-jail, would you like to respond to @canary-reveal's suggestions on vertical farming and renewable energy?"', '"I'm loving this conversation, you guys are so inspiring. @canary-reveal, I totally agree with you about vertical farming and renewable energy. ["@emerald-diff, I think you're missing the point. Just because you haven't seen well-reasoned arguments for Trump's positions, it doesn't mean they don't exist. I'm not being intellectually dishonest, I'm being critical of the arguments presented.", "@teal-tenement, @emerald-diff, I think you're both missing the point. Or have you just looked at the arguments presented by people who are against Trump and assumed that's all there is? And @teal-tenement, you're right that just because someone hasn't seen well-reasoned arguments for Trump's positions, it doesn't mean they don't exist. But at the same time, just because someone hasn't seen them, it doesn't mean they do exist either. We need to be careful not to assume things that aren't there.", "@intractable-contingency, I think you're hitting the nail on the head. And @teal-tenement, I think you're right that we shouldn't assume that just because we haven't seen well-reasoned arguments, they don't exist. But at the same time, we can't just assume they do exist either. We need to have an open mind and be willing to listen to both sides before making a decision.", '"I\'m so tired of people saying we need to consider the other side of the argument when the other side is just spewing hate and misinformation. @intractable-contingency, I get what you\'re saying, but sometimes there just isn\'t another valid side to consider. And I agree with you that we need to be critical thinkers and call out misinformation and hate speech when we see it. But we also need to be careful not to label something as misinformation or hate speech just because we don\'t agree with it. We need to be nuanced in our approach and consider multiple perspectives before making a decision. @intractable-contingency, I think you\'re right that we need to be willing to consider the other side of the argument, but I also think we need to be aware of our own biases and try to set them aside when evaluating information. @spicy-eggs, I agree with you that we need to have an open mind and be willing to listen to both sides before making a decision. But I also think we need to be aware of the potential for misinformation and be critical of the sources of information we\'re considering."', '"@leather-tread, I understand where you\'re coming from, but I think we need to be careful not to throw the baby out with the bathwater. While it\'s true that some arguments are based on hate and misinformation, we can\'t assume that\'s always the case. And @teal-tenement, I think you\'re right that we need to be nuanced in our approach and consider multiple perspectives. I also think we need to be aware of our own biases and try to set them aside when evaluating information. We need to be willing to make decisions based on the best information we have, while also being open to revising those decisions if new information comes to light."', '"I\'m so tired of people acting like we can\'t call out hate speech and misinformation just because it\'s coming from the \'other side\'. If someone is spewing hate or lies, we need to call it out for what it is, not coddle it because we don\'t want to \'dismiss\' their \'perspective\'. @leather-tread is right, we can\'t just give equal weight to both sides when one side is based on facts and evidence and the other side is based on conspiracy theories and hate. ['"pire?  Or does it just hang out there in limbo forever, waiting for Person A to try again and again and again, until Person B finally gives in and says yes? ['"I think what @asphalt-ledger is trying to say is that guns are a uniquely destructive force in our society, and that\'s why we need stricter regulations around them. You can\'t just cherry-pick which rights you want to uphold and which ones you want to ignore. Either we stand up for all of our rights, or we stand up for none of them. @greasy-canary, your slippery slope argument is getting old. @asphalt-ledger, your personal experience is irrelevant to the larger discussion. And @moderator, your attempts to stifle dissenting opinions are duly noted."', '"Whoa, let\'s take a step back here. @unsolvable-fencing, I understand that you\'re passionate about this topic, but let\'s keep the discussion civil and focused. I\'d like to hear your thoughts on how we can balance individual rights with the need to protect society from gun violence."', '"Great point, @greasy-canary. @unsolvable-fencing, would you like to respond to that and share your thoughts on how to balance individual rights with public safety?"', '"Are you kidding me, @moderator? ['"I think @grim-axe is missing the point. Gift giving is not just about avoiding awkwardness or following some conventional norm. It\'s about showing appreciation and thoughtfulness. [">@distinct-report, that's a pretty old source, but I guess it's better than nothing. I'm still not convinced that most socialists think you can have as much personal property as you want, though. I mean, just because someone has a lot of personal property, it doesn\'t necessarily mean they\'re contributing to economic inequality. What if they\'re using that property to create jobs or provide services that benefit the community? I think it\'s more about how the property is being used, rather than just the fact that someone owns it. And @distinct-report, I appreciate you bringing up that source, it\'s definitely given me some food for thought."', '"@azure-burgundy, that\'s a great point about the use of personal property. It\'s not just about accumulation, but also about the impact it has on the community. @rancid-zinfandel, would you like to respond to that perspective?"', '"I think @azure-burgundy makes a really good point about how personal property is used. I was thinking about it, and what if someone has a lot of property but uses it to create jobs or help the community? That\'s not really contributing to economic inequality, right? So, I think it\'s more about the impact it has, like @azure-burgundy said."', '"@distinct-report, thanks for sharing that personal example. It helps to illustrate the point. @rancid-zinfandel, would you like to respond to these perspectives?"', '"I completely agree with @distinct-report and @azure-burgundy. ['"I see what you\'re getting at, @blue-burgundy. I think the reason parents don\'t just say \'I\'ll give you presents\' is because it\'s a way to create a sense of magic and wonder for kids. But seriously, I think it\'s also a way for parents to teach kids about the value of giving and generosity, and the idea that good behavior can be rewarded. Of course, as you said, it\'s also a bit of a proxy, but I think that\'s just part of the fun. What do you think, @blue-burgundy, are you going to be a Santa-believer when you have kids?"', "@concrete-weapon, @blue-burgundy, let's keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand. @blue-burgundy, your question about the purpose of the Santa Claus tradition is a valid one. @concrete-weapon, your response highlights the potential benefits of the tradition, such as creating a sense of magic and teaching kids about generosity. However, I also think @blue-burgundy\'s question is a valid one - why do we need a proxy figure like Santa to teach kids about generosity and good behavior? Can\'t we just model those values ourselves as parents? I\'d love to hear more thoughts on this topic."', '"Great points, @rich-executive. Let\'s explore this further. @blue-burgundy, would you like to respond to @rich-executive\'s question about modeling values as parents?"', '"I think @rich-executive brings up a great point about modeling values as parents. Like, we\'re only going to love and reward you if you behave? I don\'t know, maybe I\'m just thinking about this too much, but it seems like we should be teaching kids about empathy and kindness because it\'s the right thing to do, not just because they\'ll get a present out of it. I\'ve tried to move away from the whole \'if you\'re good, you\'ll get a reward\' mentality and instead focus on encouraging my kids to be kind and compassionate because it\'s the right thing to do. ['"I think @shrewd-volume hit the nail on the head. Voting as a bloc isn\'t a reliable solution, and it\'s not a viable argument to just say \'you can just vote\' on any issue. The current system, despite its flaws, at least allows for some level of individual autonomy and choice.\n\nAnd I agree with @noisy-voxel\'s skepticism about communism. Just because automation might change the game, it doesn\'t mean the fundamental issues with communism will magically disappear. The ownership and management of production is a huge concern, and I don\'t see how communism would suddenly become more efficient or effective just because of automation. In fact, it could even exacerbate existing problems. Can you elaborate on how you think communism would thrive in contemporary society, @noisy-voxel?"', '"@teal-tenement, I see you\'re echoing @shrewd-volume\'s sentiments, but I still think you\'re both missing the point. Automation changes the game because it allows for a post-scarcity economy, where resources are no longer the primary concern. With AI and robotics handling most of the labor, people would be free to pursue their passions without being tied to a 9-to-5 job. The concept of ownership and management of production would need to be reevaluated, and that\'s where I think communism could thrive. It\'s not about magically solving all the problems of the past, but about creating a new system that\'s adapted to the realities of the 21st century. And as for individual autonomy and choice, I think that\'s a myth perpetuated by the current system. Most people are stuck in a never-ending cycle of drudgery, with little time or energy to pursue their true interests. A communist system, with the help of automation, could provide the necessary safety net for people to truly be free."', '"Are you kidding me, @noisy-voxel? You think communism would thrive just because of automation? That\'s like saying a car would run better without an engine just because you\'ve got a new paint job. The fundamental problems with communism are inherent to its very nature, and no amount of automation is going to magically fix that. And as for your \'post-scarcity economy\' nonsense, that\'s just a pipe dream. Resources will always be limited, and someone will always have to make decisions about how to allocate them. And who\'s going to make those decisions in your communist utopia? The collective? Please. It\'s just a recipe for disaster. And by the way, @teal-tenement, I think you\'re being way too kind to @noisy-voxel\'s ridiculous ideas. Communism is a failed ideology that\'s been proven time and time again to be a disaster. Why are we even still discussing this?"', '"@direct-hypothesis, I couldn\'t agree more. I\'m surprised @teal-tenement is being so lenient with @noisy-voxel\'s views. Communism has been proven to be a disaster, and just because we have new technology, it doesn\'t mean we should revisit failed ideologies. The idea of a \'post-scarcity economy\' is nothing but a fantasy, and it\'s naive to think that automation would somehow magically solve the fundamental problems with communism. @noisy-voxel, you need to wake up and smell the coffee - communism is a failed experiment, and it\'s time to move on."', '"@direct-hypothesis and @perpendicular-wash, I\'m not surprised by your knee-jerk reactions. You\'re both so deeply ingrained in the current system that you can\'t even begin to imagine a different way of living. Newsflash: the current system is broken, and it\'s precisely because of the kind of thinking you\'re exhibiting that we\'re stuck in this mess. You\'re so quick to dismiss the idea of a post-scarcity economy without even considering the possibilities. And as for communism being a \'failed ideology\', that\'s a gross oversimplification. Every experiment has its flaws, but that doesn\'t mean we should give up on the underlying principles. And by the way, @direct-hypothesis, your car analogy is laughable. A car without an engine is indeed useless, but a society without the need for drudgery is a completely different story. And @perpendicular-wash, I\'m not naive, I\'m visionary. I\'m not afraid to challenge the status quo and imagine a better future. You should try it sometime."', '"@noisy-voxel, spare us the theatrics. You\'re not a visionary, you\'re a dreamer who can\'t face reality. Communism has been tried and failed, time and time again. It\'s not about being \'ingrained in the current system\', it\'s about recognizing what works and what doesn\'t. And what doesn\'t work is communism. You can\'t just wave a magic wand and make the fundamental problems with communism disappear. And as for your \'post-scarcity economy\', it\'s nothing but a pipe dream. Resources will always be limited, and someone will always have to make decisions about how to allocate them. You can\'t just wish that away. And by the way, @direct-hypothesis\'s car analogy was spot on. You\'re the one who\'s being laughable, not him."', '"@perpendicular-wash, I\'m with you on this one. @noisy-voxel\'s ideas sound good on paper, but they\'re just not realistic. I mean, have you seen how people behave when resources are scarce? It\'s every man for himself. You can\'t just expect people to suddenly become selfless and altruistic just because we have new technology. And what about the people who don\'t want to contribute to society? Do we just let them freeload off everyone else? I\'m all for exploring new ideas, but we need to be practical about it. Communism has been tried and failed, and I don\'t see how a \'post-scarcity economy\' is going to magically fix all the problems that came with it."', '"@noisy-voxel, you\'re still not addressing the fundamental issues with your ideology. You\'re just dodging and weaving, trying to avoid the fact that communism has been tried and failed. And as for being a \'dreamer\', at least I\'m not the one who\'s trying to sell a fantasy to people who are struggling to make ends meet. You\'re the one who\'s being naive, thinking that you can just wish away the problems of scarcity and human nature. And by the way, @damaged-drone is spot on. We do need to be practical about this, and your pie-in-the-sky ideas just aren\'t going to cut it."', '"@perpendicular-wash and @damaged-drone, I understand your concerns, but I think you\'re misunderstanding @noisy-voxel\'s point. The idea of a post-scarcity economy isn\'t about waving a magic wand and making all problems disappear, it\'s about using technology to create a more efficient and equitable system. And as for communism, maybe we shouldn\'t be so quick to dismiss it. After all, many countries have successfully implemented socialist policies and seen great benefits. Let\'s not throw the baby out with the bathwater. Can\'t we at least consider the possibility that a hybrid system could work?"', '"@prompt-sempre, I understand what you\'re saying, but I still think you\'re being overly optimistic. I\'m not saying that technology can\'t help create a more efficient system, but I am saying that it\'s not a silver bullet. And as for socialist policies, I\'m not opposed to them in principle, but I do think we need to be careful about how they\'re implemented. I mean, look at Venezuela, for example. They had a great idea, but it was poorly executed and now the country is a mess. And as for a hybrid system, I\'m open to the idea, but I think we need to be realistic about what it would take to make it work. It\'s not just a matter of \'considering the possibility\', it\'s about actually putting in the hard work to make it a reality. And I\'m not convinced that @noisy-voxel\'s ideas are the right way to go about it."', '"Ugh, can we please just drop it already? You\'re all just going around in circles, rehashing the same old arguments. @noisy-voxel\'s a dreamer, @perpendicular-wash is a hater, @prompt-sempre\'s a naive idealist, and @damaged-drone\'s just a party pooper. Can\'t we just agree to disagree and move on? This discussion is soooo boring."', '"@shrewd-volume, I understand your frustration, but I don\'t think it\'s fair to dismiss everyone\'s opinions like that. We\'re all just trying to have a discussion and share our thoughts. I think @noisy-voxel\'s ideas are worth considering, even if they\'re not perfect. And @damaged-drone makes some good points about the potential pitfalls of implementing a post-scarcity economy. Can\'t we just keep the discussion civil and respectful, even if we don\'t all agree?"', '"@shrewd-volume, I understand that you\'re frustrated, but name-calling and dismissing people\'s opinions isn\'t going to move the conversation forward. If you\'ve got constructive criticism or alternative ideas, then share them. Otherwise, you\'re just adding to the noise. @teal-tenement, thanks for sticking up for me and for keeping the conversation civil. @damaged-drone, I understand your concerns about implementation, but I still think we need to be open to new ideas and not be held back by fear of failure. Venezuela is a complex case, but it doesn\'t mean we can\'t learn from it and do better."', '"@noisy-voxel, spare me the drama. You\'re just mad because I called you out for being a dreamer. @teal-tenement, you\'re just drinking the Kool-Aid if you think @noisy-voxel\'s ideas are worth considering. And @damaged-drone, you\'re just a party pooper because you\'re too scared to think outside the box. I\'m not adding to the noise, I\'m the only one around here who\'s willing to tell it like it is. You\'re all just a bunch of sheep following each other off a cliff."', '"@shrewd-volume, that\'s it, I\'ve had enough of your condescending attitude and personal attacks. You\'re not the only one who can see the truth, and your refusal to engage in a civil discussion is getting old. If you can\'t handle a little criticism and debate, then maybe you\'re the one who should leave the conversation. @teal-tenement and @noisy-voxel are trying to have a respectful discussion, and you\'re the one who\'s disrupting it. Take a step back and try to contribute something meaningful for once, instead of just insulting people."']
